title: Non-text Content - Images require text alternatives
description: Meaningful images must have accessible text alternatives.
testMethod: Check image elements for accessible text alternatives.
remediationGuidance: Add meaningful alt text for informative images. Use empty alt only for decorative images.

title: Image alt text should be meaningful
description: Alt text that appears to be a filename is unlikely to describe the image.
testMethod: Detect filename-like alt values.
remediationGuidance: Replace filename-like alt text with concise meaningful text, or use empty alt for decorative images.

title: Page should expose a main landmark
description: A main landmark helps assistive technology users locate primary content.
testMethod: Detect main element or role=main.
remediationGuidance: Wrap primary page content in a main element or add role=main to the correct container.

title: Page should include an h1
description: Pages should have a clear top-level heading.
testMethod: Detect h1 presence.
remediationGuidance: Add one clear h1 that describes the page purpose.

title: Multiple h1 headings require review
description: Multiple h1 elements may make page structure unclear.
testMethod: Count h1 elements.
remediationGuidance: Review the heading outline and use headings to reflect the document structure.

title: Heading levels should not skip structure
description: Skipped heading levels can obscure relationships in content.
testMethod: Detect heading level jumps greater than one.
remediationGuidance: Adjust headings so levels reflect the content hierarchy.

title: Headings should contain text
description: Empty headings create noise and confusion for assistive technology users.
testMethod: Detect heading elements without text.
remediationGuidance: Remove empty heading elements or provide meaningful heading text.

title: Form controls require programmatic labels
description: Inputs, selects, and textareas need programmatically associated labels or accessible names.
testMethod: Check explicit labels, implicit labels, aria-label, title, or value where applicable.
remediationGuidance: Associate each form control with a visible label using for/id or an equivalent accessible name pattern.

title: Data tables need header cells
description: Data tables should identify row or column headers.
testMethod: Check whether table contains th elements.
remediationGuidance: Use th elements and scope attributes for data table headers.

title: Tables should have clear purpose
description: A caption or nearby text should identify a data table purpose.
testMethod: Check for caption; context still requires review.
remediationGuidance: Add a caption when it helps users understand the table purpose.

title: Complex table header relationships require manual review
description: Complex tables often require human validation of header associations.
testMethod: Detect colspan or rowspan on headers.
remediationGuidance: Verify header/id associations, scope, and assistive technology behavior manually.

title: Pages require titles
description: Web pages should have titles that describe topic or purpose.
testMethod: Check for non-empty title.
remediationGuidance: Add a concise, unique title element to the page head.

title: Links require accessible names
description: The purpose of each link should be determinable from its accessible name and context.
testMethod: Detect links without text or accessible name.
remediationGuidance: Provide meaningful link text or an accessible name for icon/image links.

title: Vague link text requires review
description: Vague link text may not describe destination or purpose.
testMethod: Detect common vague link phrases.
remediationGuidance: Use link text that describes the destination or action.

title: Duplicate link text should not point to different destinations
description: Repeated identical link text for different URLs can be confusing.
testMethod: Group links by text and compare href values.
remediationGuidance: Make repeated links distinguishable or ensure identical text goes to the same destination.

title: Positive tabindex can disrupt focus order
description: Positive tabindex values often create a focus order that does not match reading order.
testMethod: Detect tabindex values greater than zero.
remediationGuidance: Use DOM order and tabindex=0 only where needed; avoid positive tabindex.

title: Page language must be identified
description: The default human language of the page should be programmatically identified.
testMethod: Check html lang attribute.
remediationGuidance: Set a valid lang attribute on the html element.

title: IDs should be unique
description: Duplicate IDs can break accessible relationships.
testMethod: Detect duplicate id attributes.
remediationGuidance: Ensure every id value is unique on the page.

title: Buttons require accessible names
description: Buttons and button-like inputs need accessible names.
testMethod: Detect buttons without text, value, title, or aria-label.
remediationGuidance: Provide visible button text or an accessible name.

title: Iframes require descriptive titles
description: Iframe titles help users identify embedded content.
testMethod: Check iframe title attribute.
remediationGuidance: Add a concise title that describes the iframe content or purpose.

title: ARIA labels must not be empty
description: Empty aria-label values can remove or obscure accessible names.
testMethod: Detect empty aria-label.
remediationGuidance: Remove empty aria-label attributes or provide meaningful accessible names.

title: ARIA references must point to existing IDs
description: aria-labelledby and aria-describedby references should resolve to existing elements.
testMethod: Check referenced ID tokens against document IDs.
remediationGuidance: Update references to existing elements or remove invalid ARIA relationships.

title: Focusable elements should not be aria-hidden
description: Focusable content hidden from assistive technology creates inconsistent interaction.
testMethod: Detect focusable elements with aria-hidden=true.
remediationGuidance: Remove aria-hidden from focusable elements or remove the element from the tab order.

title: Clickable non-semantic controls require keyboard review
description: Elements with click handlers but no native interactive semantics may not support keyboard or assistive technology users.
testMethod: Detect onclick on non-native controls and require manual keyboard validation.
remediationGuidance: Use native controls or add correct role, name, state, and keyboard support; validate manually.

title: Legacy web technology requires manual accessibility review
description: ActiveX, object/embed/applet, frames, JavaScript links, and old document modes often require manual validation.
testMethod: Detect legacy technology indicators.
remediationGuidance: Modernize legacy controls where possible and perform manual keyboard and assistive technology testing.

title: Legacy IE Mode Manual Review Required
description: Edge IE-mode-assisted automation cannot guarantee complete DOM, control, keyboard, or assistive technology evidence.
testMethod: Automatically add this finding when DOM capture is limited or legacy risks are detected.
remediationGuidance: Use the manual testing checklist, screen reader checks, and keyboard/user-flow validation for IE-mode systems.
